questions.question: What does AI stand for?
questions.answer: Artificial Intelligence

questions.question: Which of these is a subset of Artificial Intelligence?
questions.answer: Machine Learning

questions.question: What does "Gen AI" primarily refer to?
options: AI used only for generating reports
AI that generates new content like text, images, or code
questions.answer: AI that generates new content like text, images, or code

questions.question: Which statement best describes the relationship between AI, ML, and DL?
options: AI is a subset of ML
DL is a subset of ML, and ML is a subset of AI
ML and DL are unrelated to AI
questions.answer: DL is a subset of ML, and ML is a subset of AI

questions.question: A "Domain Expert + Gen AI" combination is valuable mainly because:
options: Gen AI replaces the need for domain expertise entirely
Domain experts write the AI's code
The domain expert validates and directs AI output with real-world context
It removes the need for prompts
questions.answer: The domain expert validates and directs AI output with real-world context

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of applying Gen AI in a real business context?
options: Using a calculator to add numbers
Using an LLM to draft customer support replies
questions.answer: Using an LLM to draft customer support replies

questions.question: Which of these is NOT one of the commonly cited core skills needed to work effectively in AI (programming, prompting, domain knowledge, data literacy, critical evaluation)?
questions.answer: Graphic design mastery

questions.question: Why is "critical evaluation of AI output" considered an essential skill rather than optional?
options: Because evaluation is only needed for image models
Because it replaces the need for testing
Because generative models can produce plausible-sounding but incorrect (hallucinated) output
questions.answer: Because generative models can produce plausible-sounding but incorrect (hallucinated) output

questions.question: In the AI implementation spectrum, which approach typically requires the least coding effort but the least customization?
options: Using a no-code/low-code AI tool or pre-built API
questions.answer: Using a no-code/low-code AI tool or pre-built API

questions.question: Which best describes "Deep Learning" relative to "Machine Learning"?
options: Deep Learning is a database technique
Deep Learning only applies to robotics
Deep Learning uses multi-layered neural networks and is a specialized subset of ML
questions.answer: Deep Learning uses multi-layered neural networks and is a specialized subset of ML

questions.question: Which symbol is used to start a single-line comment in Python?
Which of these is a valid Python data type for storing decimal numbers?
questions.answer: float

questions.question: What will len([1, 2, 3]) return in Python?
What is the key difference between a list and a tuple in Python?
options: Tuples can hold more data types than lists
Lists are mutable, tuples are immutable
Tuples are always faster but mutable
questions.answer: Lists are mutable, tuples are immutable

questions.question: Which construct is used to handle exceptions in Python?
questions.answer: try/except

questions.question: What is the output of: print({1, 2, 2, 3, 3, 3})?
options: Error, sets can't have duplicates added

questions.question: In a Python virtual environment workflow, what is the primary purpose of requirements.txt?
options: To list the exact package dependencies needed to recreate the environment
To compile Python to machine code
questions.answer: To list the exact package dependencies needed to recreate the environment

questions.question:
What does this function return?
def add(a, b=5):
    return a + b
print(add(3))
Which statement about Python's `with open(file) as f:` pattern for file handling is true?
options: It only works for reading, not writing
It automatically closes the file when the block ends, even if an exception occurs
It opens the file but never closes it
It requires manually calling f.close() afterward, or it will error
questions.answer: It automatically closes the file when the block ends, even if an exception occurs

questions.question: What does RPA stand for?
questions.answer: Robotic Process Automation

questions.question: Which Python library is commonly used to automate mouse and keyboard actions on the desktop?
questions.answer: PyAutoGUI

questions.question: Playwright is primarily used to automate:
questions.answer: Web browsers

questions.question: Which PyAutoGUI capability is typically used to locate an image on the screen and click it?
questions.answer: locateOnScreen() combined with click()

questions.question: What is a key risk of screen-coordinate-based automation (like basic PyAutoGUI scripts)?
options: It breaks easily if screen resolution or window position changes
It is immune to UI changes
It can never run on Windows
questions.answer: It breaks easily if screen resolution or window position changes

questions.question: Compared to PyAutoGUI, why is Playwright generally more reliable for automating web pages?
options: It only works on mobile devices
It interacts with DOM elements directly rather than relying on screen pixel coordinates
questions.answer: It interacts with DOM elements directly rather than relying on screen pixel coordinates

questions.question: In Playwright, which approach is the recommended way to wait for an element to appear before interacting with it?
options: Using time.sleep() as the sole reliable method
questions.answer: Using built-in auto-waiting / page.wait_for_selector()

questions.question: What is a key advantage of Playwright's auto-waiting mechanism over manual time.sleep() calls?
options: It removes the need for selectors entirely
It waits intelligently for elements to be actionable, reducing flaky tests and unnecessary delays
It disables JavaScript on the page
questions.answer: It waits intelligently for elements to be actionable, reducing flaky tests and unnecessary delays

questions.question: When automating a real desktop application end-to-end with PyAutoGUI, what's the recommended practice before run-time clicking?
options: Confirm reference images/coordinates match the current screen state (e.g. via screenshot)
Hardcode coordinates once and never verify again
Skip testing and run directly in production
questions.answer: Confirm reference images/coordinates match the current screen state (e.g. via screenshot)

questions.question: Which of these tasks is better suited to Playwright than PyAutoGUI?
options: Automating form submission and data scraping on a website
questions.answer: Automating form submission and data scraping on a website

questions.question: What does API stand for?
questions.answer: Application Programming Interface

questions.question: Which HTTP method is typically used to retrieve data from a REST API?
questions.answer: GET

questions.question: What tool is commonly used to manually test API endpoints by sending requests?
questions.answer: Postman

questions.question: In REST APIs, which HTTP method is generally used to create a new resource?
questions.answer: POST

questions.question: What is a key advantage of FastAPI over many traditional Python web frameworks?
options: Built-in automatic interactive API documentation (Swagger UI) and high performance via async support
questions.answer: Built-in automatic interactive API documentation (Swagger UI) and high performance via async support

questions.question: In Streamlit, which function is used to display text or markdown content on the page?
questions.answer: st.write() or st.markdown()

questions.question: What does a REST API returning HTTP status code 404 indicate?
options: The requested resource was not found
questions.answer: The requested resource was not found

questions.question: In FastAPI, how are request body parameters typically validated automatically?
options: Using Pydantic models for type validation
questions.answer: Using Pydantic models for type validation

questions.question: Why does Streamlit rerun the entire script from top to bottom on every user interaction (like a button click)?
options: It doesn't rerun; only the called function reruns
Because it compiles to JavaScript first
Because of its reactive execution model, which re-evaluates the script to reflect updated widget state
questions.answer: Because of its reactive execution model, which re-evaluates the script to reflect updated widget state

questions.question: What is the purpose of st.session_state in Streamlit?
options: To replace the need for variables
To store data permanently in an external database
To persist values across reruns of the script within a user's session
questions.answer: To persist values across reruns of the script within a user's session

questions.question: What does LLM stand for?
questions.answer: Large Language Model

questions.question: Which of these is an example of a closed-source LLM accessible mainly via a paid API?
questions.answer: GPT-4

questions.question: What is Hugging Face primarily known for?
options: Hosting a large hub of open-source AI models and datasets
questions.answer: Hosting a large hub of open-source AI models and datasets

questions.question: What is the main practical difference between an open-source LLM and a closed-source LLM?
options: Open-source models can typically be downloaded, inspected, and run/fine-tuned locally; closed-source models are accessed only via the provider's API
questions.answer: Open-source models can typically be downloaded, inspected, and run/fine-tuned locally; closed-source models are accessed only via the provider's API

questions.question: What is Ollama primarily used for?
options: Running open-source LLMs locally on your own machine via a simple command-line interface
questions.answer: Running open-source LLMs locally on your own machine via a simple command-line interface

questions.question: Why might a company purchase API keys for a closed-source LLM instead of using an open-source one?
options: Because open-source models are illegal to use
To get access to a provider-hosted, often more capable model without managing infrastructure
Because closed-source models are always free
Because API keys remove the need for prompts
questions.answer: To get access to a provider-hosted, often more capable model without managing infrastructure

questions.question: LLM benchmarks (e.g., MMLU, HumanEval) are primarily used to:
options: Compare model performance on standardized tasks like reasoning, knowledge, or coding
Measure the physical size of the GPU used
questions.answer: Compare model performance on standardized tasks like reasoning, knowledge, or coding

questions.question: What does "pulling a model" mean in the context of Hugging Face or Ollama?
options: Reporting a bug in the model
Downloading the model's weights/files to your local environment for use
Deleting a model from the cloud
questions.answer: Downloading the model's weights/files to your local environment for use

questions.question: Which factor most directly affects whether you can run a given open-source LLM locally on your machine?
options: Whether you have a Hugging Face account
Available RAM/VRAM relative to the model's parameter size
questions.answer: Available RAM/VRAM relative to the model's parameter size

questions.question: When evaluating which open-source model to use for a specific task, which combination of factors matters most?
options: Only the number of GitHub stars
Task-relevant benchmark scores together with license terms and hardware requirements
Only the company that released it
questions.answer: Task-relevant benchmark scores together with license terms and hardware requirements
